data.content:
I can see you have a .NET 8 MSTest project with a failing test — `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m`.

I'll start `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run tests whenever you save a file:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` to set up hot reload for iterating on their tests. I can see the project is a .NET 8 MSTest project. The second test (`CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15`) is failing because the method returns `10.00m` for "express" but the test expects `15.00m`.

Let me set up `dotnet watch test` for them. This will automatically re-run tests when files change.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-e741b27e6d1e4e0ea0ed076ad24fa575 && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — failed (expected `15.00`, got `10.00`)

The watcher is active in shell `watch`. Every time you save `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs` (or any source file), it will automatically rebuild and re-run the tests. Just edit the `"express"` case in `CalculateShipping` to return `15.00m` instead of `10.00m` and save — the watcher will pick it up instantly.
